assertions.text: V1 was entering the roadway from a parking spot.
V1 was attempting to make a U-turn to head west.
The roadway is a five-lane, two-way roadway.
V2 was traveling east in lane two of the five-lane roadway.
As V1 made the U-turn, the left side of V1 made contact with V2's front plane.
As the vehicles continued in motion after initial contact, the left side of V1 struck the front of V2.
There was contact between V1's left side and V2's front plane followed by the left side of V1 striking the front of V2 (implying at least one initial and a subsequent contact while moving).
V1 came to final rest in westbound lanes of travel.
V2 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway.
The impacts involved V1's left side and V2's front (i.e., a left-side-to-front collision).

verdicts.evidence: The claim that V1 was entering from a parking spot cannot be determined from V2 telemetry alone; no channel records other vehicles' positions or curb/parking status (unverifiable).
V2 telemetry does not record V1's intended maneuver; steering or heading of V2 cannot confirm that V1 was attempting a U-turn to head west (unverifiable).
Roadway geometry (five-lane, two-way) is not encoded in V2's sensor channels or EDR summary; cannot verify from telemetry (unverifiable).
V2 speed and steering show motion but do not encode lane number or compass heading; cannot confirm V2 was traveling east in lane two (unverifiable).

Impact direction between V1 left side and V2 front cannot be determined unambiguously from device-frame delta-V without documented axis alignment; delta-V shows a dominant longitudinal change of [ -18.0 km/h ](T1) and lateral of [ -5.0 km/h ](T1) but axis mapping to vehicle left/right/front/back is undocumented, so the specific left-side-to-front contact is unverifiable.

The narrative of sequential contacts cannot be resolved from V2 telemetry alone; the crash pulse shows cumulative longitudinal delta-V final [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral delta-V peak [ -5.0 km/h ](T2) but does not indicate multiple distinct contact events (unverifiable).

EDR records a single event ("num_events_recorded": [1](T1)) and the delta-V traces show a single crash pulse with cumulative values [ -18.0 km/h ](T1) long and [ -5.0 km/h ](T1) lat; this does not support distinguishing an initial and subsequent contact, so the claim is unverifiable.
Final rest positions (V1 in westbound lanes) are external scene facts not recorded by V2 telemetry; cannot verify from sensors (unverifiable).
Final rest location of V2 (middle of the roadway) is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry or EDR summary; unverifiable.

While the crash pulse shows a dominant longitudinal delta-V of [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral delta-V peak of [ -5.0 km/h ](T2), axis alignment to vehicle front vs left side is undocumented, so identifying the collision as left-side-to-front is unverifiable.

result.rationale: V2 telemetry records a single crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V [ -18.0 km/h ](T1) and lateral peak [ -5.0 km/h ](T1), but device-frame axes and scene-level details (lane, direction, parking, multiple contacts) are not available; most narrative assertions about V1, lane positions, and sequence cannot be decided from V2 sensors alone.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
